etymology.text:
Unknown. Probably onomatopoeic.
Likely formed from imitative sounds and movements associated with chickens. Compare دِجْ دِجْ (dij dij, expression used to call a chicken) and دَجْدَجَ (dajdaja, “to call a chicken; (of a chicken) to run”), which appear primary and sound-symbolic. A similar pattern occurs in بَسَّة (bassa, “cat”) beside بِسْ بِسْ (bis bis).
Similar derivations may include دَجَّ (dajja, “to walk slowly, to move back and forth”) and Najdi Arabic داج (dāj, “to wander aimlessly”), both reminiscent of the animal's movement.
A similar form exists in Proto-Turkic *takïgu, though any connection is uncertain.
